data.title: Panasonic LUMIX S 40mm F2 targets everyday shooting
data.incipit: Compact full-frame prime delivers natural perspective, bright aperture and portability. Panasonic has expanded its full-frame L-Mount lens range with the LUMIX S 40mm F2, a compact standard prime aimed at...
data.body: Compact full-frame prime delivers natural perspective, bright aperture and portability. Panasonic has expanded its full-frame L-Mount lens range with the LUMIX S 40mm F2, a compact standard prime aimed at photographers who want an everyday focal length without adding much bulk to their kit. Announced in Wiesbaden on 21 April 2026, the new lens is positioned as a natural partner for street photography, portraits and landscapes, with Panasonic describing its 40mm angle of view as close to the field of view of the human eye. That makes it a slightly unconventional but potentially very appealing option for photographers who want something more flexible than a classic 50mm and a touch tighter than 35mm. The headline feature is the bright F2 maximum aperture, which should help in two key areas. First, it promises softer background blur and stronger subject separation for portrait and detail work. Second, it gives users more room to keep shutter speeds up in lower light without forcing ISO settings too high. Panasonic is clearly pitching the lens as a practical creative tool rather than a niche specialist optic, and that is reinforced by its small dimensions and light weight. At roughly 40.9mm long and approximately 144g, it is designed to stay out of the way in daily use while still offering the benefits of a full-frame prime. That compactness matters even more in the context of the current LUMIX S system. Panasonic says the lens pairs especially well with the LUMIX S9, creating a lightweight setup that should suit travel, day-to-day carry and more casual shooting. It also shares a similar profile with the collapsed LUMIX S 18-40mm F4.5-6.3 and uses the same 62mm filter diameter, which could make it an easy addition for existing S9 users. Beyond stills, Panasonic has also paid attention to video use. The company says the lens includes focus breathing suppression and micro-step aperture control, both intended to support smoother, more polished footage. There is also a control ring and focus button for more direct handling, while a dust- and splash-resistant design and fluorine-coated front element should help the lens cope better with changing weather and regular use. Panasonic’s roadmap graphic included on page three of the press materials also points to further lens development, including a future wide prime and a large-aperture telephoto zoom, suggesting continued momentum for the LUMIX S system around compact full-frame shooting. Panasonic LUMIX S 40mm F2 Specifications Mount: L-Mount Focal length: 40mm Maximum aperture: F2 Lens type: Full-frame standard prime Length: Approx. 40.9mm Weight: Approx. 144g Filter thread: 62mm Weather sealing: Dust- and splash-resistant Front element coating: Fluorine Video features: Focus breathing suppression, micro-step aperture control Controls: Control ring, focus button Panasonic LUMIX S 40mm F2 Price and Availability The LUMIX S 40mm F2 is scheduled to go on sale from the beginning of June at a recommended retail price of £349.
